---
title: "Gridded Data"
author: Andy P. Barrett
date: last-modified
format:
  html:
    code-fold: true
jupyter: python3
---

_Placeholder_

# Overview

Gridded data is data that is organized as an array with two or more dimensions.  For geospatial data, the dimensions of two-dimensional gridded data are spatial (e.g. $x$ and $y$ coordinates, or latitude and longitude). Additional dimensions are often altitude or depth, and time.

_For reference_
https://carpentry.library.ucsb.edu/ucsb-geospatial/01-intro-raster-data/index.html
